component.name: PQC Today Hub
component.description: PQC algorithm and product registry for post-quantum migration planning

components.name: BTQ Bitcoin Quantum
components.description: First quantum-safe fork of Bitcoin. Testnet replaces ECDSA with NIST-standardized ML-DSA signatures to protect against quantum threats. Increased block size to 64 MiB to accommodate larger PQC signatures (38-72x larger than ECDSA). Full transaction lifecycle with quantum-resistant cryptography. Protects ~6.26M BTC in addresses with exposed public keys against Harvest Now Decrypt Later attacks.

components.name: Hitachi DoMobile
components.description: DoMobile Ver.5 (Jan 2026) integrates 01 Quantum IronCAP PQC using FIPS 203 (ML-KEM). First in Japan.

properties.name: pqctoday:fips_validated
properties.value: No (implements FIPS 203 algorithm; no NIST CMVP validation)

components.name: Project Eleven Solana PQC
components.description: First post-quantum testnet for Solana blockchain developed in collaboration with Solana Foundation. Replaces EdDSA signatures with NIST-standardized ML-DSA demonstrating quantum-resistant transactions are practical on high-throughput blockchain architectures (65000+ TPS). Open-sourced testnet with readiness assessments and migration test environments. $20M Series A funding announced January 2026.

components.name: SEALSQ Quantum Shield
components.description: Post-quantum semiconductor-based trust anchors deployed in U.S. systems. QS7001 and QVault-TPM post-quantum secure processors incorporating quantum-resistant algorithms and hardware roots of trust. Protection against harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks. Millions of devices deployed worldwide. Joint venture with Kaynes SemiCon in India for PQC chip manufacturing.

components.name: Forward Edge-AI Isidore Quantum
components.description: Hardware-based plug-and-play post-quantum encryption platform. NSA CNSA 2.0 compliant protocol-agnostic solution working with legacy and modern networks. Features one-way data diode with NIST-approved PQC algorithms including ML-KEM. Tested by U.S. military branches with SpaceX deployment. Secures data in transit without software rewrites or PKI dependencies.

components.name: QuSecure QuProtect R3
components.description: First end-to-end cryptographic security platform for quantum era. Provides cryptographic discovery (Recon) crypto-agility engine (Resilience) and real-time compliance reporting. Enables dynamic cipher key and algorithm updates without code changes or downtime. Works across cloud on-premise and edge environments. Includes QuEverywhere for mobile PQC.

components.name: SandboxAQ AQtive Guard
components.description: Platform for automated discovery and inventory of cryptographic assets. Facilitates transition to post-quantum cryptography and enhances cyber readiness against AI-powered attacks. Provides continuous visibility into cryptographic dependencies. Deployed in U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) CIO systems. HQC algorithm contributor selected by NIST.

components.name: 01 Quantum IronCAP
components.description: Quantum-safe cryptographic engine using Modern McEliece, a patented optimization of Classic McEliece with Goppa code-based encryption, plus NIST-approved PQC algorithms and hash-based digital signatures. Not standard Classic McEliece. Powers Hitachi DoMobile Ver.5 remote access system in Japan. Developing Quantum AI Wrapper (QAW) for securing AI data with Full Homomorphic Encryption and Quantum Crypto Wrapper (QCW) for blockchain quantum-safety.

components.name: Bouncy Castle C# .NET
components.description: Implements HQC (Hamming Quasi-Cyclic) and ML-KEM for .NET platform. Provides quantum-resistant key exchange mechanisms with good performance characteristics on .NET runtime.

components.name: Bouncy Castle Java
components.description: Comprehensive PQC implementation supporting ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and HQC (Hamming Quasi-Cyclic). Provides quantum-resistant key encapsulation and digital signatures through JCE/JCA provider. Supports hybrid classical/PQC modes for gradual migration.

components.name: Bouncy Castle Java LTS
components.description: Long-term support release with ML-DSA composite signatures, enabling hybrid quantum-classical signature schemes. Allows organizations to use both traditional (RSA/ECDSA) and post-quantum signatures simultaneously for defense-in-depth.

components.name: OpenSSL

algorithmProperties.parameterSetIdentifier: ML-DSA-44, ML-DSA-65, ML-DSA-87, ML-KEM-1024, ML-KEM-512, ML-KEM-768

components.description: Full support for NIST-standardized PQC algorithms including ML-KEM (Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism) for key exchange, ML-DSA (Module-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Algorithm) for signatures, and SLH-DSA (Stateless Hash-Based Digital Signature Algorithm). Enables quantum-safe TLS 1.3 connections with hybrid mode support.

components.name: Google Tink
components.description: Multi-language library (C++, Go, Java). Experimental PQC access available in C++. HPKE (Hybrid Public Key Encryption) support is shipping in stable releases. NIST PQC algorithm integration (ML-KEM, ML-DSA) in progress but not yet in stable releases. Used in Google infrastructure and Cloud KMS. Project restructured into language-specific repos (tink-java, tink-go, etc.).

components.name: Botan
components.description: Supports hybrid post-quantum key exchange in TLS 1.3 using ML-KEM or FrodoKEM. Includes post-quantum signature schemes: ML-DSA (Dilithium), SLH-DSA (SPHINCS+), HSS/LMS, XMSS. Includes post-quantum key encapsulation schemes: ML-KEM (Kyber), FrodoKEM, Classic McEliece.

components.name: SafeLogic CryptoComply
components.description: FIPS 140-3 validated cryptographic modules. Supports hybrid mode (classical + PQC) and includes CryptoComply PQ TLS for quantum-safe data in transit. Drop-in OpenSSL compatibility. CryptoComply Go v4.0 adds all NIST PQC algorithms (ML-KEM FIPS 203 ML-DSA FIPS 204 SLH-DSA FIPS 205). PQC capabilities rolled out across Core v3.5 (OpenSSL-based) Mobile v3.5 Java v4 and Go v4.

components.name: SAP Cryptographic Library
components.description: Version 8.6 enables quantum-safe TLS 1.3 handshake using hybrid ECDHE-MLKEM key agreement based on X25519MLKEM768. ML-KEM is a standardized FIPS-203 algorithm for key encapsulation, preventing session key decryption by quantum computers.

components.name: PQShield PQSDK
components.description: Comprehensive SDK for PQC integration. Features PQCryptoLib-Core (FIPS 140-3 certified) and PQCryptoLib-Soft. Supports mobile, server, and embedded.

components.name: Go stdlib crypto/mlkem
components.description: Native ML-KEM-768 and ML-KEM-1024 since Go 1.24 (Feb 2025). Hybrid post-quantum TLS enabled by default since Go 1.24 combining classical elliptic-curve cryptography with ML-KEM. Go 1.26 adds crypto/hpke and crypto/mlkem/mlkemtest packages with derandomized encapsulation for known-answer tests. FIPS 203 compliant.

components.name: RustCrypto ml-kem
components.description: Implements ML-KEM (formerly Kyber), a post-quantum secure key encapsulation mechanism standardized by NIST in FIPS 203. Supports three security levels: MlKem512 (128-bit), MlKem768 (192-bit), and MlKem1024 (256-bit). Implementation is pure Rust, version 0.2.3. Security warning states it has never been independently audited.

components.name: RustCrypto ml-dsa
components.description: Implements ML-DSA (formerly CRYSTALS-Dilithium) per FIPS 204 final. Supports parameter sets MlDsa44 (Category 2), MlDsa65 (Category 3), and MlDsa87 (Category 5). Implementation is pure Rust, supports `no_std`, and includes key generation, signing, and verification. Warning: Never independently audited; use at own risk.

components.name: RustCrypto slh-dsa
components.description: Implements Stateless Hash-based Digital Signature Algorithm (SLH-DSA) based on SPHINCS+, finalized in NIST FIPS-205. Supports 12 parameter sets (SHA2 and SHAKE variants at L1, L3, L5 security). Signatures range from 7KB to 50KB. Implementation is not independently audited; requires Rust 1.75+.

components.name: mlkem-native
components.description: Implementation of ML-KEM (FIPS 203) with support for security levels 512, 768, and 1024. Includes formal verification (CBMC, HOL-Light) for memory safety, type safety, and constant-time execution on AArch64 and x86_64. Supports native backends for Arm, Intel/AMD, and RISC-V. Used as a default implementation in libOQS, AWS-LC, and rustls.

components.name: leancrypto
components.description: The library exclusively contains PQC-resistant algorithms. It implements NIST-approved schemes: ML-DSA/Dilithium, ML-KEM/Kyber, SLH-DSA/Sphincs+, and BIKE. Accelerated implementations exist for x86_64, ARMv7/ARMv8, and RISCV64. Algorithms are tested with NIST ACVP and hold official CAVP certificates.

components.name: Cosmian covercrypt
components.description: Post-quantum secure with a 128-bit security level. Supports hybridization using INDCPA-KYBER (INDCPA version of NIST standard Kyber). Data encrypted with AES-GCM 256-bit. Uses Curve25519 for classic coordinates and INDCPA-Kyber for hybridized coordinates.

components.name: pqm4
components.description: Library contains implementations of post-quantum key-encapsulation mechanisms and signature schemes targeting ARM Cortex-M4. Includes schemes standardized by NIST (FIPS 203, 204, 205), selected for standardization, part of NIST Round 4, additional signatures Round 1, and KpqC Round 2. Integrates clean implementations from PQClean.

Security Test module emulates tens of thousands of concurrent users with realistic application traffic while measuring PQC-specific KPIs: handshake latency, SA establishment time, re-keying overhead, key size impact. Supports hybrid scenarios (PQC initiator plus classical responder) to validate backward compatibility. Key finding: IKEv2 SA establishment increases from 38ms (classical) to 12626ms with some PQC configurations. Partnership with QNu Labs for QKD/QRNG/hybrid PQC test badging. [Note: release date predates FIPS 203/204/205 finalization (Aug 2024); may reference draft/pre-standard algorithms.]

Version 7.0.2 (Oct 2025) supports hybrid key exchange simulation with Cloudflare endpoint. Stateful traffic generation at 1; 10; 100; 400 Gbps. Community Edition available free. components.name: Keysight Inspector

Dilithium TVLA data generator and Known Key Analysis module validate ML-DSA implementations against power; timing; and electromagnetic side-channel leakage. ML-KEM and SLH-DSA support in pre-release. Captures millions of power traces per second using PXI-based DS1050A testbench. Collaborated with NXP on ML-DSA countermeasure validation and PQShield on industry readiness. Supports FIPS 140-3 Level 4 compliance verification for semiconductor and device vendors.

components.name: CryptoNext COMPASS Network Probe
components.description: Passive high-performance cryptographic data detection system deploying on network taps and SPAN ports. Identifies all cryptographic algorithms; key types; and certificate details across 100+ IT and OT protocols in real time without packet injection. First EU company to achieve NIST CAVP certification for all three finalized PQC algorithms (ML-KEM/FIPS 203; ML-DSA/FIPS 204; SLH-DSA/FIPS 205) in June 2025. On path to FIPS 140-3 module certification. Integrates with COMPASS Analytics Platform for governance reporting. Recommended by NIST NCCoE for cryptographic discovery in PQC migration workflows. Secure boot; hardened OS; integrity checks; PQC-secured TLS 1.3 management communications.

components.name: pqcscan
components.description: High-performance open-source PQC readiness scanner written in Rust. Two scan modes: tls-scan (probes HTTPS/TLS endpoints for PQC algorithm support; TLS version; cipher suite; hybrid key exchange detection) and ssh-scan (probes SSH servers for PQC host key and key exchange support). Outputs JSON with HTML report generation. Binary releases for Linux; macOS; Windows. Database filtering for detecting specific PQC algorithm OIDs. Developed by Anvil Secure. [Note: release date predates FIPS 203/204/205 finalization (Aug 2024); may reference draft/pre-standard algorithms.]

components.name: pqc-flow
components.description: Detects PQC and hybrid algorithm usage in live network traffic and PCAP captures. SSH detection: sntrup761x25519-sha512@openssh.com (OpenSSH 9.0+), mlkem, kyber, ntruprime variants via custom SSH_MSG_KEXINIT parser. TLS 1.3 detection: X25519Kyber768 (Chrome 0x11ec), X25519Kyber768Draft00 (Cloudflare 0xfe31/0x6399), NIST ML-KEM drafts (0x2001-0x2010) via custom ClientHello/ServerHello supported_groups and key_share extension parser. QUIC detection via TLS-in-QUIC handshake parsing. IKE/IPsec via nDPI classification. PQC flags bitmask tracks KEM presence, signature presence, hybrid negotiation, offered-only, and session resumption states. No nDPI patches required for SSH/TLS PQC extraction.

components.name: PQC-LEO
components.description: Open-source PQC benchmarking and evaluation framework automating computational and network performance measurement for NIST PQC algorithms. Uses OpenSSL with OQS provider to run standardized TLS 1.3 performance tests across x86 and ARM architectures. Measures: handshake latency; key generation time; signing/verification overhead; memory footprint; energy consumption. Generates comparative analysis between classical; hybrid; and pure PQC configurations. Designed for academic research and enterprise pre-deployment evaluation. [Note: release date predates FIPS 203/204/205 finalization (Aug 2024); may reference draft/pre-standard algorithms.]

components.name: Crucible
components.description: Conformance testing harness for ML-KEM (FIPS 203) and ML-DSA (FIPS 204) cryptographic library implementations. Tests real-world vulnerabilities that evade both NIST Known Answer Tests and formal verification tools. ML-KEM: 78 tests across 6 categories (compression algorithms; NTT operations; coefficient bounds validation; decapsulation logic; serialization; sampling distributions). ML-DSA: 51 tests across 6 categories (norm checks; arithmetic operations; signing determinism; verification robustness; serialization; timing consistency). Uses a language-agnostic JSON line protocol enabling harness support for C; C++; Go; Java; Rust; and other languages. Ships with harnesses for 15 production implementations including CIRCL; liboqs; AWS-LC; Go stdlib; and Bouncy Castle. Created by Symbolic Software.

components.name: IBM z16 Crypto Express 8S
components.description: IBM Crypto Express 8S (CEX8S) is the hardware security module integrated into IBM z16 mainframes. CEX8S operates in EP11 (IBM Enterprise PKCS#11) mode supporting quantum-safe algorithms including Kyber (ML-KEM) and Dilithium (ML-DSA) via the IBM Quantum Safe Cryptography API. Achieved FIPS 140-3 Level 4 certification (the highest level available). EP11 firmware provides quantum-safe key generation, encapsulation/decapsulation, and signing operations. Used in IBM LinuxONE and IBM Z as the hardware root of trust for quantum-safe workloads. IBM Quantum Safe Toolkit integrates with CEX8S for crypto discovery and migration.

components.name: AWS Certificate Manager
components.description: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) provisions and manages TLS/SSL certificates for AWS services. ACM-issued certificate endpoints use hybrid ML-KEM TLS key exchange (via AWS-LC FIPS 140-3 validated module) protecting certificate delivery in transit. AWS Private CA (ACM Private CA), the companion service, supports ML-DSA-44/65/87 (FIPS 204) certificate issuance for private PKI since June 2025, enabling quantum-safe code signing, mutual TLS, and device identity workflows. Public ACM certificates (issued via ACM free tier) currently use classical RSA/ECDSA; PQC public certificate issuance depends on CA/Browser Forum ballot adoption. ACM integrates with AWS KMS for private key storage with FIPS 140-3 Level 3 HSM backing.

components.name: Cosmian KMS
components.description: Full ML-KEM-512/768/1024 (FIPS 203) ML-DSA-44/65/87 (FIPS 204) and SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) in production since v5.12+. KMIP 2.1 server. Hybrid KEM combiner (classical + PQC via SHA-256). Covercrypt attribute-based encryption. Only open-source KMIP server with full NIST PQC algorithm suite shipping today.

components.name: SOPS (Secrets OPerationS)
components.description: PQC-capable via age encryption backend with -pq flag (hybrid X25519+Kyber768 key encapsulation). SOPS is encryption-backend agnostic; age v1.3+ post-quantum hybrid keys encrypt secrets at rest. Additional PQC KEMs available via age plugin system (age-plugin-sntrup761x25519). Supports AWS KMS Google Cloud KMS Azure Key Vault age and PGP backends.

components.name: Certicom Code Signing and Key Management Server
components.description: Quantum-resistant code-signing server using ML-DSA (formerly Dilithium); supports OTA firmware updates and SBOM signing. Integrates with NXP S32G secure boot for automotive use cases.

components.name: BlackBerry QNX Hypervisor 8.0 for Safety
components.description: Safety-certified embedded hypervisor with PQC-protected secure boot. Every QNX-based device validates a quantum-safe signature at boot. Used in adopted by Ford, BMW, Tesla.

components.name: Hex Trust Institutional Custody
components.description: Institutional digital asset custody using cold/frozen/ZeroKey wallet architectures. No published PQC roadmap as of 2026-04.

components.name: Komainu Custody
components.description: Institutional digital asset custody with bankruptcy-remote on-chain segregation. Uses MPC and HSM wallet technology. No published PQC roadmap.

components.name: Metaco Harmonize
components.description: Institutional digital asset orchestration platform (custody + trading + tokenization + staking + DeFi). Used by Citi, BBVA, BNP Paribas, HSBC. Owned by Ripple. No published PQC roadmap.

components.name: Descope Customer & Agentic Identity Platform
components.description: No-code CIAM with passwordless flows, MFA, federation, and Agentic Identity Hub 2.0 for AI agents/MCP servers. Uses standards-based identity (OAuth 2.1, WebAuthn, passkeys). PQC posture not published.

components.name: Stytch Authentication Platform
components.description: B2B and Consumer authentication platform with passwordless, passkeys, MFA, B2B SSO, organisation management. Frontend + backend SDKs (iOS, Android, React Native, JS). PQC posture not published.

components.name: Cisco AI Defense (formerly Robust Intelligence Platform)
components.description: AI Validation + AI Firewall guardrails for production AI applications. Algorithmic red teaming, threat intelligence, policy mappings. Acquired by Cisco Oct 2024. PQC posture not published.

components.name: Galileo AI Observability and Eval Platform
components.description: GenAI evaluation and production guardrails platform. Galileo Evaluate (offline evals), Galileo Protect (production guardrails), Luna foundation models, Agent Analytics. Used by Comcast, Twilio. PQC posture not published.

components.name: BeyondTrust Pathfinder Platform
components.description: AI-driven PAM platform spanning Privileged Access Management, ITDR, Cloud Identity Management, CIEM. Includes Password Safe, Privileged Remote Access, Endpoint Privilege Management, Entitle, Identity Security Insights. PQC posture not published.

components.name: DocuSign Intelligent Agreement Management (eSignature + CLM)
components.description: eSignature + Contract Lifecycle Management platform. Drafting, approval routing, signing, storage, obligation tracking, AI-augmented review. Cryptography for digital signatures relies on CA-backed PKI. PQC posture not published.

components.name: AnyDesk Remote Desktop
components.description: Remote desktop with proprietary DeskRT codec. Currently uses RSA-2048 asymmetric key exchange and AES-256 transport encryption — RSA-2048 is NOT quantum-safe and will need PQC migration. No published PQC roadmap as of 2026-04.

components.name: OpenSSL 3.5.0
components.description: First OpenSSL release with native ML-KEM, ML-DSA, and SLH-DSA support without the OQS provider. X25519MLKEM768 hybrid key exchange enabled by default in TLS 1.3. Released April 29, 2025.

components.name: Utimaco Athos
components.description: Payment HSM with PQC on roadmap. Utimaco SecurityServer general-purpose HSM has announced PQC roadmap for NIST algorithms. Athos payment-specific HSM firmware does not yet publicly list ML-KEM or ML-DSA support. German vendor positioning for European PCI DSS and EMV compliance.

components.name: Atalla AT1000e
components.description: Payment HSM. The Atalla AT1000 (current model, succeeding AT1000e) is now manufactured and sold by Utimaco (not OpenText). Has FIPS 140-3 L3 certification and integrates with OpenText Voltage SecureData. The Utimaco GP HSM Se-Series supports PQC (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, LMS, XMSS), but the AT1000 payment HSM firmware's PQC status is unclear. Organizations should evaluate migration to PQC-ready alternatives.

properties.name: pqctoday:fips_validated
properties.value: Yes (FIPS 140-3 L3 via Atalla ACS #4992) (classical algorithms only; PQC not in FIPS scope)

components.name: UEFI Forum Secure Boot
components.description: No PQC algorithm support in current UEFI Secure Boot specification (UEFI 2.10). Signature database supports RSA-2048 SHA-256 only. However, AMI achieved the industry's first working PQC implementation in Aptio V UEFI Firmware (November 2025). CNSA 2.0 mandates PQC firmware signing as the first use case starting 2025. Doug Flick (Microsoft) added as TianoCore EDK2 CryptoPkg maintainer for PQC (Feb 2026). Future revisions expected to add ML-DSA/SLH-DSA.

components.name: Windows Secure Boot
components.description: No PQC firmware signature verification in current Secure Boot. CNG supports ML-KEM/ML-DSA for applications but Secure Boot chain still uses RSA-2048.

components.name: Oracle TDE
components.description: Oracle Database 26ai introduces quantum-resistant encryption for Transparent Data Encryption. Includes support for post-quantum cryptographic algorithms for data-at-rest protection. Key management via Oracle Key Vault (OKV) with classical key wrapping pending PQC upgrade. Significant milestone as first major RDBMS to ship PQC-capable TDE. Note: ML-KEM/ML-DSA are for TLS in-transit encryption (Oracle 26ai). TDE data-at-rest uses AES-256 (quantum-resistant symmetric).

properties.name: pqctoday:fips_validated
properties.value: Yes (FIPS 140-2 via OKV) (classical algorithms only; PQC not in FIPS scope)

components.name: Thunderbird + OpenPGP
components.description: BSI-funded PQC@Thunderbird project has produced a working PQC implementation. Thunderbird 144 includes fixes for PQC signed mail handling. Uses RNP library for OpenPGP; full PQC dependent on IETF PQC-in-OpenPGP standard finalization.

components.name: Microsoft Outlook S/MIME
components.description: Windows CNG has ML-KEM/ML-DSA but Outlook S/MIME has not shipped PQC certificate support. Dependent on CA ecosystem issuing PQC X.509 certificates.

components.name: DocuSign
components.description: Uses RSA-2048/4096 and SHA-256. No public PQC announcements. PQC transition depends on PKI ecosystem supporting PQC signature algorithms.

components.name: Entrust Signing Automation
components.description: PQC capabilities in nShield HSMs. ML-DSA and SLH-DSA integration expected through HSM backend upgrades. No confirmed production PQC signing deployment.

components.name: ForgeRock Identity Cloud
components.description: Enterprise CIAM platform (now part of Ping Identity / Thales group). PQC roadmap includes quantum-safe TLS for authentication endpoints and hybrid PQC signatures for OAuth/OIDC tokens. Merger with Ping Identity accelerates access to enterprise PQC cryptography resources.

components.name: Quantum Bridge Subsea QKD
components.description: QKD-as-a-service platform targeting subsea and long-distance fiber deployments. Uses Trusted Node architecture to extend QKD beyond single fiber spans. Provides quantum-safe key exchange integrated with PQC (ML-KEM) in hybrid mode for government and critical infrastructure. Focused on satellite-ground and metro QKD networks. Participation in European Quantum Internet Alliance (QIA) projects.

components.name: STMicroelectronics ST33G1M2 TPM
components.description: ST33G1M2 is a TCG TPM 2.0-compliant secure microcontroller (CC EAL4+ certified). Current firmware supports RSA-2048 and ECC P-256/P-384. STMicroelectronics has announced the successor ST33KTPM with PQC-protected firmware and ML-KEM/ML-DSA/XMSS/LMS support — claiming to be first to provide quantum resistant features across all product ranges. The ST33G1M2 itself does not have PQC. PQC firmware upgrade for this specific model is on roadmap targeting 2026-2027.

components.name: NXP SE050 Secure Element
components.description: NXP SE050 is a plug-and-trust secure element providing ECC P-256/P-384/P-521, RSA-2048/4096, and AES-128/256. CommonCriteria EAL6+ certified. No PQC support. NXP SE051 (successor) includes ML-KEM and ML-DSA on its feature roadmap. Widely deployed in IoT, automotive, and payment systems.

components.name: BlackBerry QNX Neutrino RTOS
components.description: QNX Neutrino is a safety-certified microkernel RTOS used in automotive (50M+ vehicles), medical devices, and industrial systems. BlackBerry QNX has announced PQC integration aligned with NIST standards and ISO 21434 automotive cybersecurity requirements. Targets ML-KEM and ML-DSA for over-the-air update signing and secure communications.

components.name: BeyondTrust Privilege Management
components.description: BeyondTrust Privilege Management provides endpoint PAM, privileged remote access, and password management. Cryptographic operations rely on TLS 1.3 for transport and AES-256 for credential storage. PQC roadmap tied to underlying platform TLS library upgrades. No dedicated PQC timeline published.

components.name: Google Workspace Identity
components.description: Google Workspace Identity includes SSO, SAML, OAuth 2.0, and LDAP services for enterprise users. Google's infrastructure already uses ML-KEM hybrid TLS on all Google services (enabled 2024). Authentication tokens and SAML assertions still use classical RSA/ECC signing. Google has not announced a specific ML-DSA migration timeline for identity tokens.

components.name: AnyDesk
components.description: AnyDesk provides remote desktop software using its proprietary DeskRT codec with TLS 1.2/1.3 transport. AnyDesk is incorporated in Germany and subject to BSI (Federal Office for Information Security) PQC guidance. No public PQC roadmap announced as of Q1 2026. Transport upgrade to ML-KEM hybrid TLS expected to follow BSI TR-02102 updates.

components.name: Galileo FT
components.description: Fintech payment and wallet infrastructure platform (crypto-to-fiat conversion). Not a dedicated crypto custodian. No PQC algorithms or quantum-safe roadmap announced.

components.name: Metaco (Ripple)
components.description: Institutional digital asset custody platform (Harmonize) acquired by Ripple in 2023 for $250M. Cross-asset custody trading and DeFi services. No PQC roadmap announced.

components.name: Hex Trust
components.description: Singapore MAS-licensed institutional custody supporting 24+ blockchain networks including Bitcoin Ethereum Solana Algorand Cosmos Polkadot Avalanche. Regulated in Dubai Hong Kong Singapore Italy France. No PQC roadmap announced.

components.name: Komainu
components.description: Nomura/CoinShares/Ledger-backed institutional custody using hybrid MPC+HSM architecture. Regulated in Jersey UK Dubai Italy Singapore. No PQC roadmap announced.

components.name: Robust Intelligence AI Firewall
components.description: AI model firewall providing runtime monitoring, adversarial input detection, and model integrity enforcement. Robust Intelligence (acquired by Cisco) is developing quantum-safe model signing using ML-DSA for continuous model integrity verification and PQC-protected telemetry pipelines to defend AI infrastructure against quantum-capable adversaries.

components.name: Thales CipherTrust Data Discovery and Classification
components.description: Enterprise data discovery and classification platform with cryptographic asset inventory capabilities. Discovers sensitive data and cryptographic material across structured and unstructured data stores. Generates Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM) for PQC migration planning. Identifies quantum-vulnerable cryptographic algorithms in data at rest and data flows. Part of the Thales CipherTrust Data Security Platform. Integrates with CipherTrust Manager for unified key and crypto policy management.

components.name: Post-Quantum Hybrid PQ VPN
components.description: Production-grade hybrid post-quantum VPN using IKEv2 with RFC 9370 extensions (the IETF-ratified standard for multiple key exchanges in IKEv2, co-authored by Post-Quantum Ltd). Supports ML-KEM-1024 + Classic McEliece hybrid KEM for defense-in-depth key establishment. Fully backward compatible with existing IPsec/IKEv2 infrastructure — no rip-and-replace required. Crypto-agile architecture allows algorithm updates without redeployment. Deployed protecting NATO communications and active conflict zone drone operations. FIPS 140-3 validation in progress.

components.name: Descope
components.description: Developer-first CIAM platform with drag-and-drop authentication flow builder. Supports OIDC SAML OAuth 2.0 passkeys and social login. No PQC support or public quantum-safe roadmap detected. SOC 2 Type II certified. Classical cryptography for all token signing and session management.

components.name: Stytch
components.description: API-first authentication platform for passwordless login OTP OAuth and session management. No PQC support or public quantum-safe roadmap detected. SOC 2 Type II certified. Issues JWTs with classical RSA/ECDSA signatures. Session tokens and API keys use classical cryptography requiring future PQC migration.

components.description: Blockchain integration and financial infrastructure platform with quantum-safe cryptography roadmap. Enables financial institutions and enterprises to integrate PQC into distributed ledger workflows, smart contract signing, and regulated financial messaging.

components.name: WEntrust KeyControl
components.description: Full PQC key management with ML-KEM ML-DSA and SLH-DSA key types. Supports encapsulation decapsulation sign and verify operations. PQ-TLS for KMIP connections. nShield 5 HSM v13.8.0 natively supports PQC with CAVP validation.

components.name: indows Server 2025
components.description: General availability of PQC algorithms in November 2025 update. Supports ML-KEM and ML-DSA via CNG and Certificate functions.

components.name: OQS-OpenSSL Provider (liboqs-provider)

algorithmProperties.parameterSetIdentifier: ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, FN-DSA, HQC, Classic-McEliece

components.description: The OQS OpenSSL provider (liboqs-provider) is the canonical bridge from liboqs to OpenSSL 3.x. It provides all NIST-standardized PQC algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, FN-DSA) plus round 4 candidates (HQC, BIKE, Classic McEliece) as an OpenSSL provider plugin. Enables PQC TLS 1.3, PQC X.509 certificates, and PQC key operations in any application using OpenSSL 3.x without code changes. Maintained by the Open Quantum Safe project.

components.name: LUKS/dm-crypt
components.description: No PQC algorithm support. LUKS2 uses AES-XTS-256 with Argon2id for key derivation. No PQC key encapsulation for master key wrapping. AES-256-XTS is quantum-resistant.

components.name: libcrux
components.description: Formally verified Rust crypto library from Cryspen. Implements ML-KEM and ML-DSA with code extracted from HACL* and verified using the hax toolchain for functional correctness and runtime safety. Hybrid KEM support via XWing and X25519MlKem768Draft00. Used as a reference implementation in PQC interoperability testing by Cloudflare and Mozilla (Firefox NSS).

components.name: Trail of Bits ml-dsa
components.description: Side-channel resistant ML-DSA (FIPS 204) implementation in Go from Trail of Bits. Supports all three parameter sets: ML-DSA-44, ML-DSA-65, and ML-DSA-87. Designed with explicit focus on constant-time execution and resistance to timing and power analysis attacks. Passed all 51 ML-DSA conformance tests in the Crucible test battery.

components.name: Venafi
components.description: Venafi TLS Protect and CodeSign Protect support experimental ML-DSA and SLH-DSA algorithms for issuing PQC certificates.

components.name: Azure Dedicated HSM
components.description: Microsoft Azure Dedicated HSM powered by Thales Luna 7 hardware (FIPS 140-2 L3). Thales Luna firmware 7.9+ adds ML-KEM and ML-DSA support; Azure Dedicated HSM customers can request firmware updates for PQC capabilities. Microsoft Azure Managed HSM (Marvell LiquidSecurity) adding PQC key types per 2029 roadmap.

components.name: InfoSec Global AgileSec
components.description: Analytics and SDK platform for crypto inventory and agility. Enables automated discovery and migration to PQC without code changes.

components.name: iOS 26 / macOS 16
components.description: System-wide PQC support including PQ3 for iMessage and HPKE with ML-KEM/ML-DSA. APIs for third-party apps via CryptoKit.

components.name: Signal Protocol
components.description: Experimental PQXDH (Post-Quantum Extended Diffie-Hellman) support. Signal's approach to quantum-resistant messaging combining X25519, Kyber, and double-ratchet protocol for forward secrecy.

components.name: OpenPGP.js
components.description: OpenPGP.js PQC PR series tracks draft-ietf-openpgp-pqc (composite ML-KEM+ECDH encryption, composite ML-DSA+ECDSA signatures, standalone SLH-DSA).

components.name: OpenMLS
components.description: OpenMLS PQ branch tracks draft-ietf-mls-pq-ciphersuites-04 (ML-KEM + ML-DSA cipher suites for MLS group messaging).

components.name: mls-rs (AWS)
components.description: mls-rs PQ track: ML-KEM-based cipher suites for MLS, tracking draft-ietf-mls-pq-ciphersuites.

components.name: mlspp
components.description: C++ reference implementation for MLS protocol; PQ work in progress aligned with draft-ietf-mls-pq-ciphersuites.

components.name: libtpms
components.description: libtpms is a C library providing TPM 2.0 functionality. Tracks TCG TPM 2.0 Library v1.83 (no PQ); v1.85 PQ support in fork branches (incl. pqctoday-tpm).

components.name: swtpm
components.description: swtpm is a libtpms-based software TPM emulator. Tracks libtpms versions; PQ via pqctoday-tpm fork branches.

components.name: pqctoday-tpm
components.description: Fork of swtpm+libtpms adding TCG TPM 2.0 v1.85 RC4 PQ commands (ML-DSA-44/65/87, ML-KEM-512/768/1024, Labeled KEM). Drives the /playground/tpm-playground workshop.
